commands.author: 100yenadmin
commands.review_summary:
The PR renders Telegram inbound text entities into Markdown before agent-body construction, updates helper/body tests, removes the old link-only helper export, and adds a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +122, Tests +22, Docs +1. Total +145 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current-main source shows `resolveTelegramInboundBody` calls `expandTextLinks`, and that helper only renders `text_link` entities, so non-link formatting entities reach the agent body as plain text.
commands.review_followup: A narrow repair can preserve the old helper export while leaving the new formatter behavior intact.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve inbound text entities
target.body:
## Summary
- render Telegram inbound formatting entities as markdown before building the agent-facing body
- preserve bold, italic, code, pre, strikethrough, underline, spoiler, and text_link entities using Telegram's original UTF-16 offsets
- add helper and inbound-body regression coverage for formatted Telegram messages

## Verification
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts
- git diff --check

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram inbound formatting entities should be preserved in the agent-facing body by rendering bold, italic, code, pre, strikethrough, underline, spoiler, and text_link entities from Telegram UTF-16 offsets into Markdown before dispatch.
- Real environment tested: Current-head local OpenClaw checkout at `5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d` using the actual Telegram `renderTelegramTextEntities` runtime helper called by `resolveTelegramInboundBody`, plus the earlier GitHub Actions Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof workflow on PR head `26ca98e760f0` with private QA enabled.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `gh run view 26072521900 --json databaseId,name,conclusion,status,url,jobs`
  2. GitHub Actions job `Run agentic native Telegram proof` in workflow run `26072521900` executed the native Telegram proof scenario selected by `mantis: telegram-visible-proof`.
  3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
  4. `pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { renderTelegramTextEntities } from "./extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts"; /* invoke bold+link and triple-backtick pre cases */'` from `/home/rev/projects/openclaw-pr-83873-current` at current PR head `5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d`.
- Evidence after fix: Live CI/job output and status:
  ```json
  {
    "name": "Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof",
    "run": 26072521900,
    "conclusion": "success",
    "status": "completed",
    "url": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26072521900",
    "jobs": [
      {"name": "Validate selected refs", "conclusion": "success", "url": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26072521900/job/76656751665"},
      {"name": "Run agentic native Telegram proof", "conclusion": "success", "url": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26072521900/job/76656891367"}
    ]
  }
  ```

Current-head local runtime output from the same renderer used by the Telegram inbound body path:

`````text
$ pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { renderTelegramTextEntities } from "./extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts"; /* invoke proof cases */'
HEAD 5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d
bold+link input: Read docs now
bold+link output: Read **[docs](https://docs.example)** _now_
pre input:
before
```
inside
pre output:
````md
before
```
inside
````
`````

This directly covers the current-head ClawSweeper ask: bold plus text_link reaches Markdown as `**[docs](https://docs.example)**`, and a Telegram `pre` entity whose content contains triple backticks is rendered with a four-backtick fence so the embedded triple-backtick run cannot close the block.

- Observed result after fix: The earlier native Telegram proof workflow completed successfully for the PR head, including selected-ref validation and the `Run agentic native Telegram proof` job. On current head `5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d`, direct invocation of the same Telegram entity renderer used before agent-body construction produced `Read **[docs](https://docs.example)** _now_` for nested bold/text_link/italic entities and used a four-backtick Markdown fence for a `pre` entity whose content contained triple backticks. The targeted vitest command also passed for the Telegram helper/body paths listed in the Verification section.
- What was not tested: I could not run a fresh current-head Mantis Telegram Desktop capture from this account; the workflow dispatch route returned `403` and the comment-triggered workflow reported this actor as `read`, so the native desktop proof remains the earlier repository workflow run. The current-head proof above is direct runtime output from the exact renderer used by the Telegram inbound body path, not a new Telegram Desktop screenshot.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#52859

## Current-head proof refresh (2026-05-19)

Pushed commit `831d938b68c225ae8beda5be12e5a9ec372db8fe` fixes the ClawSweeper `pre` fence blocker by choosing a Markdown fence longer than any backtick run in the Telegram `pre` content, while preserving the existing language-fence behavior.

Commands run at that head:
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts` -> 3 test files passed, 183 tests passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.

Independent diff review passed with no security concerns or logic errors.

## Current-head proof refresh (2026-05-24)

Latest PR head tested: `bbd308644cb3a3eda410df0faf74dd2abaa92193` (empty CI-rerun commit; code under test is unchanged from parent `8d86aefe0b6febc65f136c0d2060cdfa61807de8`).

Commands run at this head:

~~~text
$ node --import tsx <<'NODE'
import { renderTelegramTextEntities } from './extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts';
const text = 'PR83873 docs now\nbefore\n```\ninside';
const entities = [
  { offset: 8, length: 4, type: 'bold' },
  { offset: 8, length: 4, type: 'text_link', url: 'https://docs.example/pr83873' },
  { offset: 17, length: 17, type: 'pre' },
];
console.log(renderTelegramTextEntities(text, entities));
NODE
PR83873 **[docs](https://docs.example/pr83873)** now
````
before
```
inside
````
~~~

Live Telegram Desktop source-message evidence was also captured from the local `Zero` bot DM after sending a formatted message from Telegram Desktop. Redacted source-message cache entry:

~~~json
{
  "source": "Telegram Desktop -> Zero bot DM -> OpenClaw Telegram sourceMessage cache",
  "head": "bbd308644cb3a3eda410df0faf74dd2abaa92193",
  "message": {
    "chat": { "type": "private" },
    "text": "PR83873 docs now\nbefore\n```\ninside",
    "entities": [
      { "offset": 7, "length": 4, "type": "text_link", "url": "https://docs.example/pr83873" },
      { "offset": 17, "length": 17, "type": "pre" }
    ],
    "link_preview_options": { "is_disabled": true }
  }
}
~~~

This proves the live Telegram Desktop path produced Telegram `text_link` and `pre` entities for the bot input, including a `pre` block whose content contains triple backticks. The current-head renderer proof above covers the exact ClawSweeper edge case for overlapping `bold` + `text_link` plus the same triple-backtick `pre` content: it renders `docs` as `**[docs](https://docs.example/pr83873)**` and chooses a four-backtick fence so the embedded triple backticks cannot close the block.

Targeted validation on current head:

~~~text
$ pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
Duration    4.47s

$ pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       188 passed (188)
Duration    23.70s
~~~

CI note: the remaining failing GitHub check at the time of this update was `checks-node-agentic-agents`, and the log shows `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timed out after 120s rather than failing an assertion. The same focused test passed locally on this PR head in 4.47s. I attempted to rerun the failed GitHub job, but GitHub returned `run 26370283600 cannot be rerun; Must have admin rights to Repository` for this account.

commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 4 constrained provider capabilities
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR keeps WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles honest by making hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed constraints visible instead of pretending they are equivalent official bot APIs. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 4 - Constrained/device-bound provider capabilities - Role: Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. - Head: `190e616d68` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `190e616d68` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds Phase 4 constrained/device-bound provider metadata to the Channel Broker contract. - Covers WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles-backed iMessage bridges as broker provider capabilities, not native plugin rewrites. - Stacks on Phase 3 official/app capability matrix (#86164), Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86114 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86148, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds `constraints` and `badges` to broker platform/provider capabilities. - Models constrained provider facts such as: - `businessApi`, `cloudApi`, `providerHosted` - `deviceBound`, `linkedDevice`, `qrPairing`, `sessionFragile` - `selfHosted`, `phoneNumberRequired`, `signalCli` - `macHostRequired`, `messagesSignedIn`, `privateApiOptional`, `privateApiRequired` - `externalBridge` - Exposes resolved provider capabilities in channel-broker status snapshots so UI/status can show constrained-provider badges. - Adds WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage target proof through `channel-broker`. - Documents constrained provider expectations in Channel Broker, SDK, WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles migration docs. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Constrained providers expose hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed limits through broker `constraints`/`badges`, and WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage still route through provider-owned broker requests. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that evaluated constrained-provider capability support and called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` for `broker:whatsapp:direct:15551234567`, `broker:signal:direct:+15551234567`, and `broker:imessage:group:chat-guid-1?threadId=imessage-thread-1`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "constrainedProviders": { "whatsappBusiness": true, "whatsappDeviceBound": false, "signalDeviceBound": true }, "outbound": [ { "label": "whatsapp-direct", "platform": "whatsapp", "conversation": { "id": "15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "signal-direct", "platform": "signal", "conversation": { "id": "+15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "imessage-group", "platform": "imessage", "conversation": { "id": "chat-guid-1", "type": "group", "threadId": "imessage-thread-1" } } ] } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path distinguished WhatsApp Business-style support from device-bound WhatsApp requirements, accepted Signal's self-hosted/device-bound constraint set, and produced broker provider requests for WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage. - **What was not tested:** Live WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, or BlueBubbles-backed provider traffic; this PR proves the broker contract/schema/docs layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/status.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not implement Phase 5 legacy-plugin freeze. - Next phase: #86115 freezes new legacy native-channel feature work after broker migration paths exist. This PR supplies the constrained-provider metadata needed before that freeze can be credible.
target.author: 100yenadmin

Independent diff review passed with no security concerns or logic errors.

## Current-head proof refresh (2026-05-24)

Latest PR head tested: `bbd308644cb3a3eda410df0faf74dd2abaa92193` (empty CI-rerun commit; code under test is unchanged from parent `8d86aefe0b6febc65f136c0d2060cdfa61807de8`).

Commands run at this head:

~~~text
$ node --import tsx <<'NODE'
import { renderTelegramTextEntities } from './extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts';
const text = 'PR83873 docs now\nbefore\n```\ninside';
const entities = [
  { offset: 8, length: 4, type: 'bold' },
  { offset: 8, length: 4, type: 'text_link', url: 'https://docs.example/pr83873' },
  { offset: 17, length: 17, type: 'pre' },
];
console.log(renderTelegramTextEntities(text, entities));
NODE
PR83873 **[docs](https://docs.example/pr83873)** now
````
before
```
inside
````
~~~

Live Telegram Desktop source-message evidence was also captured from the local `Zero` bot DM after sending a formatted message from Telegram Desktop. Redacted source-message cache entry:

~~~json
{
  "source": "Telegram Desktop -> Zero bot DM -> OpenClaw Telegram sourceMessage cache",
  "head": "bbd308644cb3a3eda410df0faf74dd2abaa92193",
  "message": {
    "chat": { "type": "private" },
    "text": "PR83873 docs now\nbefore\n```\ninside",
    "entities": [
      { "offset": 7, "length": 4, "type": "text_link", "url": "https://docs.example/pr83873" },
      { "offset": 17, "length": 17, "type": "pre" }
    ],
    "link_preview_options": { "is_disabled": true }
  }
}
~~~

This proves the live Telegram Desktop path produced Telegram `text_link` and `pre` entities for the bot input, including a `pre` block whose content contains triple backticks. The current-head renderer proof above covers the exact ClawSweeper edge case for overlapping `bold` + `text_link` plus the same triple-backtick `pre` content: it renders `docs` as `**[docs](https://docs.example/pr83873)**` and chooses a four-backtick fence so the embedded triple backticks cannot close the block.

Targeted validation on current head:

~~~text
$ pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
Duration    4.47s

$ pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       188 passed (188)
Duration    23.70s
~~~

CI note: the remaining failing GitHub check at the time of this update was `checks-node-agentic-agents`, and the log shows `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timed out after 120s rather than failing an assertion. The same focused test passed locally on this PR head in 4.47s. I attempted to rerun the failed GitHub job, but GitHub returned `run 26370283600 cannot be rerun; Must have admin rights to Repository` for this account.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve inbound text entities
target.body:
## Summary
- render Telegram inbound formatting entities as markdown before building the agent-facing body
- preserve bold, italic, code, pre, strikethrough, underline, spoiler, and text_link entities using Telegram's original UTF-16 offsets
- add helper and inbound-body regression coverage for formatted Telegram messages

## Verification
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts
- git diff --check

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram inbound formatting entities should be preserved in the agent-facing body by rendering bold, italic, code, pre, strikethrough, underline, spoiler, and text_link entities from Telegram UTF-16 offsets into Markdown before dispatch.
- Real environment tested: Current-head local OpenClaw checkout at `5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d` using the actual Telegram `renderTelegramTextEntities` runtime helper called by `resolveTelegramInboundBody`, plus the earlier GitHub Actions Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof workflow on PR head `26ca98e760f0` with private QA enabled.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `gh run view 26072521900 --json databaseId,name,conclusion,status,url,jobs`
  2. GitHub Actions job `Run agentic native Telegram proof` in workflow run `26072521900` executed the native Telegram proof scenario selected by `mantis: telegram-visible-proof`.
  3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
  4. `pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { renderTelegramTextEntities } from "./extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts"; /* invoke bold+link and triple-backtick pre cases */'` from `/home/rev/projects/openclaw-pr-83873-current` at current PR head `5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d`.
- Evidence after fix: Live CI/job output and status:
  ```json
  {
    "name": "Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof",
    "run": 26072521900,
    "conclusion": "success",
    "status": "completed",
    "url": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26072521900",
    "jobs": [
      {"name": "Validate selected refs", "conclusion": "success", "url": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26072521900/job/76656751665"},
      {"name": "Run agentic native Telegram proof", "conclusion": "success", "url": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26072521900/job/76656891367"}
    ]
  }
  ```

Current-head local runtime output from the same renderer used by the Telegram inbound body path:

`````text
$ pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { renderTelegramTextEntities } from "./extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts"; /* invoke proof cases */'
HEAD 5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d
bold+link input: Read docs now
bold+link output: Read **[docs](https://docs.example)** _now_
pre input:
before
```
inside
pre output:
````md
before
```
inside
````
`````

This directly covers the current-head ClawSweeper ask: bold plus text_link reaches Markdown as `**[docs](https://docs.example)**`, and a Telegram `pre` entity whose content contains triple backticks is rendered with a four-backtick fence so the embedded triple-backtick run cannot close the block.

- Observed result after fix: The earlier native Telegram proof workflow completed successfully for the PR head, including selected-ref validation and the `Run agentic native Telegram proof` job. On current head `5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d`, direct invocation of the same Telegram entity renderer used before agent-body construction produced `Read **[docs](https://docs.example)** _now_` for nested bold/text_link/italic entities and used a four-backtick Markdown fence for a `pre` entity whose content contained triple backticks. The targeted vitest command also passed for the Telegram helper/body paths listed in the Verification section.
- What was not tested: I could not run a fresh current-head Mantis Telegram Desktop capture from this account; the workflow dispatch route returned `403` and the comment-triggered workflow reported this actor as `read`, so the native desktop proof remains the earlier repository workflow run. The current-head proof above is direct runtime output from the exact renderer used by the Telegram inbound body path, not a new Telegram Desktop screenshot.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#52859

## Current-head proof refresh (2026-05-19)

Pushed commit `831d938b68c225ae8beda5be12e5a9ec372db8fe` fixes the ClawSweeper `pre` fence blocker by choosing a Markdown fence longer than any backtick run in the Telegram `pre` content, while preserving the existing language-fence behavior.

Commands run at that head:
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts` -> 3 test files passed, 183 tests passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.

Independent diff review passed with no security concerns or logic errors.

Latest PR head tested: `bbd308644cb3a3eda410df0faf74dd2abaa92193` (empty CI-rerun commit; code under test is unchanged from parent `8d86aefe0b6febc65f136c0d2060cdfa61807de8`).

Commands run at this head:

const text = 'PR83873 docs now\nbefore\n```\ninside';
const entities = [
  { offset: 8, length: 4, type: 'bold' },
  { offset: 8, length: 4, type: 'text_link', url: 'https://docs.example/pr83873' },
  { offset: 17, length: 17, type: 'pre' },
];
console.log(renderTelegramTextEntities(text, entities));
NODE
PR83873 **[docs](https://docs.example/pr83873)** now
````
before
```
inside
````
~~~

Live Telegram Desktop source-message evidence was also captured from the local `Zero` bot DM after sending a formatted message from Telegram Desktop. Redacted source-message cache entry:

~~~json
{
  "source": "Telegram Desktop -> Zero bot DM -> OpenClaw Telegram sourceMessage cache",
  "head": "bbd308644cb3a3eda410df0faf74dd2abaa92193",

    "chat": { "type": "private" },
    "text": "PR83873 docs now\nbefore\n```\ninside",
    "entities": [
      { "offset": 7, "length": 4, "type": "text_link", "url": "https://docs.example/pr83873" },
      { "offset": 17, "length": 17, "type": "pre" }
    ],
    "link_preview_options": { "is_disabled": true }

$ pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts --reporter=verbose
